text: I have been a long time customer.  I have had other providers and T Moble by far had been the best.

Lousy phone company when they get you as a customer what they do is rob you for a garbage service, bad signal, bad data and lousy attention, over costs in the bills that even they do not know how to explain. Do not be robbed, there are many operators that for a fifth of what these mafiosos charge, they give you a quality service.

TMobile is childish and unprofessional I had to call several times because reps would sit in the phone and laugh not just that T-Mobile is the reason my Info is on the dark web I don't recommend doing business with this company they're bs

I signed up for service online and ordered a phone. My daughter decided to put me on her service, family plan since it would be easier on me since I'm on disability and have limited income. I called T-Mobile to cancel and they said that since I haven't received the phone yet when the driver arrives with it, to refuse the phone and they will cancel the service, no problem. That is what I did. I received a bill in the mail for almost $200! I called them and my daughter called them. They said to go to the store in Evansville, IN to tell them and they would handle it. I went to the store and they said to talk to the corporate office about it! I made two payments with two checks, in two months. In the amount owed. Now they sent me another bill saying that I hadn't paid the last bill for $71.58 and now I owe them an additional $7! I have a canceled check that proves I paid. I guess I will have to get the BBB involved. My advice is always to pay by check so you can rub it in their faces that you paid them! And keep your receipts for at least three years in case they try to take you to court for nonpayment. I have so many businesses to do that because if they can get you to pay twice to recover losses in other areas, they will. Do not use T-Mobile. Their customer service is non-existent and they use people that have a thick foreign accent that you can't understand them. When you ask them to speak slower, they ignore you. I plan on looking long and hard for a new service, but from the other reviews on here about other carriers, it's very depressing. And yes, they need to redo the stars to have a "no star" option on here.

I scheduled a payment arrangement for $195. The payment didn’t go through from my Navy Federal account so I paid $195 from a different account. What I didn’t know is even though I paid , they tried a second time to take the payment out of Navy Federal and it went through. So making it a total of $390 within 3 days. After calling customer support to ask for 1 payment to be returned, they said they couldn’t do it because there was a balance on my account. I’m so angry because they know this is not right! So they are keeping the extra $195 and they don’t give a crap about my bank account being in the negative because of them resubmitting a payment that I already took care of…. Shame on you T-Mobile!!! But again they don’t give a Crap about their own customers!!

It's weird that T-Mobile isn't rated much higher here. I won't ditz the other cell phone carriers I've used because it's in poor taste, but I will sing T-Mobile's praises. Definitely a superior brand! As a rule, they hire nice people, and their tower network performs exceptionally well.
CAVEAT: If you want a bargain basement price, you'll need to bring your own unlocked phone and sign up for one of the "Connect by T-Mobile" plans. (All the plans that include a free phone cost much more.)

I would give T-Mobile negative stars. I return the equipment within a week to remain with current provider. I received email they received the equipment back. They kept charging my bank card for a service I never used for equipment I don’t have. I ended up calling them to cancel everything. They charge my bank card again. The problem is I ended up stopping my card. They couldn’t pull money out of my account. Now, they have collection agency after me. I called them and was lied to. The guy was disrespectful on the phone.
the company is useless they don't have the time to train their employees the right way that's way they do mistakes  now I have to pay 2000 dollars for those mistakes
INTERESTING TO NOTE THAT MOBILE DOES NOT CONSIDER NURSES AND DOCTORS ELIGIBLE FOR THERE FIRST RESPONDER PLAN ..VERY POOR AND SAD..

Been with them for 3 years.  Have been treated like trash.  I broke my phone, got a revvl 6x pro 5g which is junk.  Had it less than 12 hours went to get different phone which customer service told me I could do.  However, the store rep wanted me to reshelf it for $100.  Phone only cost 189 to 229.  No one informed me of reshelving fees.  So I am stuck with a phone no blue tooth for zoom calls which I have to have for doctors, disconnects calls, overheats, doesn't charge until after 4 hours, drops calls and the speaker doesn't work.  I get to keep this piece of crap for 2 years.  I submitted this section on April 9, 2024

Here's a little update as of April 11, 2024. I was informed to stop calling customer service on this issue so they screwed me over on this phone they have no intention of calling me back and making it right please stay away from T-Mobile.
Worst customer service ever. I have trouble accessing my home internet account, called twice and got yelled at both times.

Company of scamers I have business accounts with those scam artist  they offerd me ipod internet and z flip they told me in promotion I don't need to pay for devices  I will pay in monthly payments  months later this new years  2023 2024 they called me from Philippines  they told me to pay for all devices  I talked to super vise I explained everything she when and listening to all conversations when I both those devices she told me I was right they was in promotion and she will take care of that  . Than after 2 hours all my lines was disconnected  .
I couldn't talk to anyone in New years lost my customers .I paid by force for all devices  and changed to prepaid  today collections agency call to collect money for mobile  . I just don't get it I paid all devices still.they want to run my credit  . Advise don't sign up with those scams

I was a Tmobile Customer almost 25 years, never late or missed a payment. Decided to go to Xfinity Mobile for a better dream.Tmobile was all nice, told me if I wanted to come back they would save my no for me!  Then in about a month I get a 480.00 bill and I am 68 years old, fixed income, and it tore my nerves up bad! I called Tmobile thinking there was a computer glitch! They informed me that about 15 years ago I had got a thing call boost device and that I had to bring it in or they were going to charge me 480.00. I didn’t even know what they were speaking of, still don’t. I said why if I owed you anything would you wait 15 plus years to tell me, they said it was because I switched to another company! I have no idea what they are talking about, I have lost two sons and a husband in that 15 years. I am so upset with a company that as a realtor I promoted , didn’t get very good service , especially outside of town but I stuck with them and this is the thanks I get! I feel if I owed them for something they should have told me 15 years ago or longer.But to say I owe them fo a booster I never heard of it and I feel so betrayed by a company I stood by for 25 years! Dont have 480.00! to pay them , am mad and broken heated to be treated in such a way! I would have maybe went back to Tmobile, but as far as I am concerned I would use a tin can to communicate, at least it wouldn’t slap me in the face and treat me like Tmobile has!  The date of my experience started in around 2005 and ended in March 2024.

If I could give negative stars I would. I canceled my services with this company over six months ago, I paid a full month of service that they did NOT provide, returned all the equipment and now, SEVEN months later I received a bill for a past due amount of $21. They will never stop billing me for stupid reasons. How can I have a past due bill from an account that does not exist? Stay away from Tmobile!!! They are the most terrible company to get services from. I left because of all the problems with billing, connection, you name it. Worst experience ever 😡

Wrong people to have service with. They abuse the options to make their time spent just for money not quality or better reviews my experience was bad refurbished devices has defective calibration and issues the service is bad not helpful to customer than lead to paying after ignored 
Not worth to keep yes pay to get yourself away from their contact they are sick bad.
They failed to activate a pre-paid e-sim. After 3 hours asked for my money back - they say this may take 72 hours!

Well let’s start with the fact that I’ve had T-Mobile for quite sometime and yet they decided to screw me over with international calls. I went overseas for work for 2 months. My phone was on WiFi calling I made sure and this isn’t my first time overseas doing this. They told me I had $434 charges in international calls and I argued with them but unfortunately they sent it to collections. So my advice to anyone is to steer clear of Tmobile and as a veteran I do not recommend them. Money hungry companies like this need to be shut down and they need to be held accountable for destroying peoples credit. Funny how phone companies don’t boost your credit yet they can destroy your credit… Seems fair.

I was trying to upgrade my phone for this S24 Ultra.  In the biggening there was a promotion trade in the phone for $1000 with a plan upgrade. or keep the plan for $500.  I was told they will honor the $500 but they will get back to me.  I had to call them back 4x since they never reach out to me.   Apparently, the promotion is over and I got screw.  I was  extremely upset with T-Mobile.  This is the second issue I had with T-Mobile, their system was compromised and my Identity was stolen.  It's been a nightmare with this phone carrier.

They have by far the WORST cell service in the country I have to drive 3 miles from my house to get one bar to be able to call someone, it’s absolutely ridiculous. I randomly lose cell service all the time even while in town. Verizon was 10x better than this garbage!

I dealt with Kevin and Demetrious at the Monroe/Talmadge location in Toledo, OH.  My 16-year-old son signed up for a phone using the T-Mobile app without parental consent.  He did not lie about his age, even used his driver’s license and social security card.  Demetrious wouldn't even listen to me, instead immediately insinuated my son lied about his age, which bothered me and when I said I didn't want to pay the $70 restock fee, he said "ok we're done here" and slid the phone across the table.  I had not raised my voice and was nothing but respectful.

Then I tried to tell him he didn't know what he was talking about because my son used his real age - and Demetrious started lecturing me on how he has a bachelor’s and master’s degree.  Demetrious then left the conversation, and for the whole time we were there waiting for them to cancel the line (after I paid the fee) Demetrious would not even look at me or acknowledge me.  I have no idea what T-Mobile is teaching their managers, but Demetrious immediately escalated the situation instead of de-escalating it. 

I also dealt with Kevin, whom I asked, "have you ever heard the term 'the customer is always right?'"  He said, "we both know that's not true."  Wow.  I will never do business with T-Mobile anywhere, and the company should be ashamed of how their employees treat customers.  But hey, Demetrious has a master’s degree y'all.  He knows better than all of us.

I spent 8 hours trying to activate a phone and port a number over.  It was finally determined the phone was defective. After being transferred to 7 different people and a whole lot of lip service I decided to just cancel everything and return their equipment.  Then the 2nd s**t show started trying to get a transfer pin number so I could have my phone number back.  They refused to release my phone number with out a text being sent to the phone. The phone was broken and did not work.  Morons..... I ended up having to go to a store to have them call and get me a transfer pin number which took another hour.  If I could give a ZERO star I would,

I wish I could give -5 stars. Unbelievably unethical behavior.

Here's my story
- I left them for Verizon. When I closed my account they said that we were all settled up and there were no other charges.
- 9 months later, I get a credit report alert that they created a new charge and sent it to collections. No phone call, text, or email as a warning.
- I call them, after 4 hours they agree that they would honor the promise originally made (that there would be no further charges) and would remove the charge. I think that's the end of it
- One year later, I get another credit alert. They didn't remove the charge, they just sat on it it
- Call them up, they accuse me of lying that this previous exchange ever happened (even though I read them the notes I made during the call from the incident) and insist that the charge is valid
- I agree to pay the ransom to make the problem finally go away ($241)

Stay away

The make promises and guarantees that they can't or know they can't.  The store salesman promise in home 5g internet.  They gave us the box.  when I set it up, there was no signal.
I called and spoke to an actual service tech on the phone that told me the near by towers were not upgraded for the new 5g technology and there were no plans to in the future...
T Mobile has poor customer service for everything you have to call them 100 times

We started our service online then had to call customer service and they sent the phones through fed ex. From the time we received the phones they never got service. I could call 611 to get to customer if I was in one spot in a driveway. 2 days of being on phone with customer service they said we had to take the phones to a T-Mobile store 21 miles away to return them since our service wouldn't work in our area. We did that they charged our bank $4 and something and now 2 weeks later charged our bank $100 for a months service. We never even had service the two plus days we had the phones. I called and they put in a request to credit it back and she said the request is pending. Then I get a letter in the mail that our bill is over due for $100. I called my bank and they said they couldn't stop the payment but if they did it again I could stop payment on another and it would cost me $35. BEWARE of T-Mobile, never again if we move again will I try them or recommend them. It cost $140 to try them out and be without phone service for 3 days. One mad customer when we live in limited social security retirement.
Update: they took another $15 out of our account, I guess $100 wasn’t good enough for 2 or 3 days of no service 😡

This company is HORRIBLE. Practically STOLE money from me and my family.  I was using t-mobile for over 10 years and was a loyal customer. Never missed 1 payment. However, they would do some sketchy things with my bill that had Me question the company’s credibility over the last few years I was with them.  

Initially, I wouldn’t check my bill statements and this was a mistake because T-Mobile would charge me for things that I never used or purchased. When I would call customer service, they would lie and say I did purchase (a plan, an equipment etc), but I know that I never had purchased anything additional.   When I would ask them to remove those things, they would confirm over the phone verbally that they would be removed from my plan- months would go by, they would remove it initially and then it will be added on again.  I was the sole authorized user on my account and so no one else had access to make any changes to my plans. 

After a few years of dealing with this, and having to stay with T-Mobile because I was under a phone contract for two years, I finally decided to leave. However, when I left, guess what happened? 

They charged me for an additional month, AFTER, I left because I had left my card on file before closing my account. When I transferred my number to another company T-Mobile automatically removed my access to all of my files, to my online account - everything, so I could not remove any of my personal information since they took my account away!  So, I tried disputing the charge and they gave me an excuse that they had to charge me because I never paid the first month of service… what?  Whatever, I couldn’t get my money back….. But then, two months later, they sent me another bill saying I owed an equipment I never returned from back in 2016. I was STUNNED!  I never purchased the equipment they said I had purchased in-store, I also never saw this “charge” on ANY of my bills throughout my time with the company. 
I was so certain I was done with T-Mobile and then they send me to collections ?? 
I had done EVERYTHING to avoid this prior to leaving. I went to the store, closed my account, received verbal and physical confirmation that everything was paid for and I received the last three month invoices to confirm it. Yet they still found a way to send me to collections for something I have no record or recollection of?  How the hell do they get away with this fraudulent behavior?  I’m not the only person I know that they have done this with. I am so upset!  I will never use T-Mobile again!! I will never recommend them to anyone.   They’re over priced, and they are fraudulent!! Once you’re ready to leave them, that’s when they want to make things even worse for you financially.   

Don’t ever use T-Mobile!! Mint Mobile has been amazing for me.  I live in NYC and it works JUST like, if not better than T-Mobile service. I pay 35$ a month and I have no unexpected fees. 

Now time for me to fight with collections over this T-Mobile bill …. Wish me luck. 

(Written by Rafael B.)

I wish I could give T-mobile a better score because they were great until I had to deal with their affiliate company Assurant for a broken phone. Assurant totally fell through in replacing my phone. So I went through 3 calls to T-mobile to get help. They did a lot of lip service how I was a valued customer but did NOT help me in any way other than offering a full price phone replacement.

In short, when I tried to add cell phones to the 5G internet service I had set up the day before, they set up a new account that was linked to the interim cell phone numbers, instead of the email address I had linked to the account the day before (I did not know I had two accounts).  I had autopay set up for the first account, which was apparently not being used. They started charging for the two phone lines before they were even able to be set up, with all of the necessary transfer actions.  They shut down my home internet service for lack of payment, even though I was paying on the first account, so I had to pay $270 to reactivate it (while I was in two online meetings needed for income) without a bill to know what I was paying for.  Subsequently, the necessary items were complete to transfer the cell phones and the only thing T-mobile was able to do was render the cell phones as bricks and make it so that they didn't work at all.  After spending over 10 hours on the phone with them, they started telling me I had to wait 24 hours for provisioning to occur, 18 hours after we first  started to try the transfer.  Ultimately, my wife needed functional phones, so then I switched to verizon.  Even though the only function T-mobile served was to kill my wife's phones, they will not refund the $160 for the cell phone services that they were not able to activate, the late fee for payment of a bill that was only sent to a nonexistent phone number, or the reactivation fee.  So they want me to pay for a service that they couldn't provide solely because I signed up for it.  I have now paid $330 to T-mobile for a partial month of a $60 service, with no recourse.  Unfortunately, at the moment, T-mobile is the only viable home internet service in the area other than Starlink.
Supervisor Cecy employee id 8993, the worst costumer service ever.

I was planning on going on the 55+ plan but changed my mind. I called T-Mobile asked if I should mail the sim cards they sent me back. They told me to destroy them. They proceeded to charge me for 3 months even though I never activated a phone through them. They said since I never closed the account they could charge me whether I had phone service or not. The 55+ plan was supposed to be $55 a month. T-Mobile charged me $76.20 a month for 3 months on a credit card I used to get the Sim Cards. I would advise anyone thinking of doing business with T-Mobile avoid them like the plague.

Customer service is garbage. When you were on the phone for an hour and a half and they do absolutely nothing to resolve your issue and you find out that they had signed you up for a plan on your eight-year-old iPad. That’s for $34.99 that has international calling and things that she will never use instead of the service that has more data Without all the extra features for $15… I still have no service at home on any of my devices and the customer service representative after an hour and a half who did nothing, hung up on me. She told me to go to the store and get new Sims cards for all of my devices. Now make that makes sense. I told her no that they would tell me to call her and she hung up..

For context, I'm currently stationed in northern Japan. Back in mid Dec 2023, all T mobile users here suffered a service outage. I called tech support and they acknowledged that there was a tower issue and they are working to resolve it. Approximately 3 weeks go by and I finally have my data back. I called customer support to discuss the possibility of getting some kind of credit since I went 3 weeks to both of my lines without any service. First she offered $50. I told her that I didn't think it was a fair compensation on a $142 bill so she agreed and offered to cover the full amount. A week goes by and no adjustment, I contacted them again and they reassured that it was being worked. A few more weeks goes by and no adjustment. I made contact again and they now tell me a person named Ronalyn had to review and approve the credit. More time goes by and I sent another message asking for Ronalyn and all they could do is send her a message asking her to give me a call back. In the meantime they credited me $20 two times for a total of $40 saying that was all they were allowed to credit me for. I called one last time in mid March 2024 and the lady I spoke had her management team review all the chat history for the last 3 months. She acknowledged about the promise of the credit however stated that it was a FALSE promise. No one on her team not even her managers could help me and that the only thing she can do is put in another message to Ronalyn from the San Anotonio dept to give me a call but even then she could promise me that she would call. 3 and half months since this issue and still not resolved. $40 credit on a $142.11 bill  when I had no service at all for 3 out of the 4 weeks, is that fair compensation? I will state that when I was with Sprint I never had issues. Tmobile has absolutely been Horrible!
Very poor coverage in San Diego and East coast.

Guys don’t even think buy things from these scammers, so I bought a plan with them I wasn’t be able to receive it, the cellphone was sent back to them and I was told that I can try again  directly from the shop, so couple weeks when I tried again  I just realize that they’ve been charging me for the plan which I didn’t even use, the worst part they made me pay 90$ for a receipt because they said that if I don’t do that, they will pull a inquiry in credit bureaus,so I paid it and they told me that they will refund me that couple weeks after, well guess what I called again and customers service which is really useless told me that I must to pay the other half, because the account is close and cancelled, but still I have to pay for something that I never used.be careful with these guys.
What's wrong? Everything is wrong with tmobile.  They are liers and don't care about YOU, The customer.  Sell junk such as Rvvls that they suppose to upgrade from my iPhone.

I was a loyal customer for 14 years who ported my line last month.  Prior to porting, I called to ask if the fees would be prorated or would I be billed for a full month regardless of the date.  The young lady confirmed it would be prorated and a refund would be issued.  Not only was the fees not reimbursed, they were also trying to charge me additional fees.  

I asked to speak to a supervisor to explain my concerns and Mark, the supervisor, was not only unpleasant but rude.  He stated it was company "standard" rules to keep the entire payment regardless of the port date.  As I continued to explain what was communicated to me, he put me on mute and returned only to repeat "company standard rules" and to let me know his decision was final.

I asked the name of rep who transferred me to which he replied he could not give out anyone's name nor could he refund any monies.

Not only was he rude, he hung up in my face.

This company cares NOTHING about people.  The service sucks and they will charge for every penny they can.  

STAY FAR AWAY FORM TMOBILE! THEY ARE NO GOOD

We switched back from Verizon and it was the worst choice. We're in a "5G high speed area" that's fully pink, but my husband gets ZERO reception at work when he's in a full coverage area and got perfect reception on Verizon. 

Nothing of mine loads even though I have full bars on a 5G network. 

And we're stuck until we pay our phones off. At this point, a little extra money on Verizon is worth it considering we pay 230 dollars a month for our phones not to work.

Also annoyed that one reason we did switch was "Netflix on us" with no ads. To get an email that oh we're going to ad supported because we're also giving you Hulu... on exactly one plan you get Hulu, so now we're paying 8 extra dollars a month plus 30 for a 3rd line we were told was free when joining, only to be told that oh you're on a military plan so that doesn't apply, even though we were clear from the beginning that it was a military plan and told it would be free. 

Overall a huge mess and the superior customer service isn't enough to keep us anymore.

If I could I'd give this poor, sorry, pathetic excuse for a phone service negative zero stars. They don't give you any advanced notice whatsoever when your upcoming bill is due; no texts, or anything, they just shut the phone off and internet without any warning. Not only that but they have the nerve to charge five dollars extra when you go to their store to pay your bill, they also have terrible customer service. Tell your family, tell your friends, your neighbors, your mailman, everyone you meet, don't go with T-Mobile. Would not recommend ever! Worst. Provider. Ever!

I was disconnected three times before being able to complete my support request with a T-Mobile representative. I inquired about an additional T-Mobile line of service for a new SyncUp Tracker. I told the representative I was calling to find out what promotions were available. The representative said if I ordered a new line of service, I would receive a new SyncUp Tracker for free. She specifically and unequivocally assured me the device would not be part of any T-Mobile financing agreement. She asked for my credit card to pay the sales tax charge. I provided her with my credit card information. The T-Mobile agent said she would email me an e-signature to sign so she could ship out the device. When the e-signature form arrived, it carried a 24-month finance agreement. I was extremely suspicious and asked the representative to explain. The representative told me, in no uncertain terms, not to worry about it. The representative said the e-signature form was the only form T-Mobile had at the time. The T-Mobile agent said that once the device shipped, the agreement would be cancelled, and I would receive the device for free, without any financing and without having to stay with T-Mobile for 24 months. I signed the agreement, knowing I had the buyer's remorse period as protection. The agent gave me her personal guarantee that I would get a free tracker without the financing agreement. After my phone with the T-Mobile agent, I called T-Mobile and spoke with several representatives as well as a billing supervisor. Each T-Mobile representative said I entered into a financing agreement. None of the subsequent agents or supervisors I spoke with about this incident cared about the fact that a T-Mobile agent had defrauded me. All anyone from T-Mobile had to do was listen to the recorded conversation I had with the dishonorable T-Mobile agent, and it would be clear that the agent deliberately lied to me.

I made multiple requests to have the representative I spoke with call me back. I never received a call.

I used to love them however after having my phone for 5 years and paying 
EVERY MONTH for the protection plan I drop my phone in toliet and its barely working. So my fiance calls Saturaday night sunday monday cuz of hos midnight shift and starts a claim. My phone will be sent off monday at 12 midnight. NOT iTS TUESDAY 930 PM AND MY PHONE may be sent out in 3 days..not holding my breathe since they cant cant find a phone. I called and cant pay more towards another phone and when  i am done on phone i tell guy6 to hang up since my phone is garbage and after my call on another phone and 10 mins later he starts talking and REFUSED to hang up. i yelled to hang up another 5 times and refused. i give them 0 stars and i used to like them. my wedding has been moved up since my mothers dying of COPD and 5 different heart issues and i cant get a phone ive been paying for ... for 5 years. IDIOTSSSS!!!!!!!!
Taylor Smith from the Tallahassee FL location, specifically Apalachee Pkwy, Suite 13, was a life saver!  He went above and beyond to assist me.  Thank You Taylor!!

I reached out to T-Mobile for a warranty exchange! They sent me a warranty device to a store! Went to the store The store would not take my device as a warranty! This happened to me three times! I was promised by T-Mobile that I would not be charged the warranty fee, because of my wasted time! I probably spent 2 hours driving back and forth to the different stores! The first warranty device they sent me arrived with a cracked screen! I then had to send it back! Now I've been charged an extra $50 on my bill. The agent Michael that I talked to wasn't willing to help at all! He wanted to give me for the credit for the device that was sent with the broken screen! Not acknowledge the promise that the representative made about my time being wasted and not having to pay that warranty fee! T-Mobile has been the worst mistake I have ever made! I will be switching back to Verizon! T-Mobile is not the best in 5G is not the best in anything!

AVOID T-MOBILE at all costs!!! Signed up to switch 6 lines from Verizon. They never gave me a 6-digit pin so couldn’t validate my account to activate the devices. Two months later I received a bill for $451.70 for service that has NEVER BEEN PROVIDED. When I called to have this corrected they again said that without the 6 digit PIN my account has not been validated so they can’t help, yet THEY HAVE BILLED ME FOR AN ACCOUNT THAT HAS NEVER BEEN VALIDATED or ACTIVATED. Unethical!!! Nikki from customer service refused to escalate and hung up on me. Said I have to go into a T-Mobile store to fix their error. Worst customer service in my life. Again, AVOID T-MOBILE AT ALL COSTS!

I recently activated my WiFi service with T-Mobile.
I visited Bufrod store and met Jeff Russo
Jeff is extremely friendly knowledgeable and explains in easy language.
7 STAR for Jeff

The absolute worst! I am simply livid right now. I was being charged for an extra line without having that line. I called last June to cancel the line, and I was told that their system was down so they could not cancel, but would do so once they got back on. Fast forward to March of 2024, looked at my bill and realize they never cancelled. Called T-mobile so they could reimburse me for the charges, I was told they could only give me 2 months and 3 as a courtesy. The manager was absolutely terrible! I am so riled up right now, have to just take a deep breath and figure out how to get my money back. Absolutely DO NOT recommend this company. SHe actually said my account was being transitioned from sprint to t-mobile last June so its truly not on them. .... shame.

Very bad customer service with t mobile And t mobile insurance I Broke  my phone. Try to make a claim for it.Meanwhile, I went to T-Mobile sto.Purchase a cheap phone. That's what they recommend till.I get my new phone that I I'm going to claim And I did not add insurance to the new phone I purchase
To get by with it till Because I'm a hairdresser.I need my phone Till the insurance Send me new phone. The moment I put on a SIM card on my cheap phone that I bought to get by till I received my other phone that I used what insurance saying That They do not cover it because my SIM card is working on the other phone I bought an automatically. The insurance was moved into it. This is the second phone I lose with T-Mobile and I think they are thieves.
 They can find the best way to steal your money. I'm disgusted.  How I've been treated today after 2 and a 1/2 hours.Ask me for My Phone gigabytes And the zip code of the store I purchased the phone from which does not Make sense.  very weird.and They make you feel.
That you're trying to steal a phone from them Disgusting Liars

I received a Galaxy Watch 4 and I received it on 3/13/24 and it stopped working on 3/14/24. I took the watch back to the T-mobile store and it would not connect to the wearable app. I sent the watch back per the Assurant service protection agent, I sent the watch back on 3/19/24 and T-mobile is giving me the run around on getting a replacement watch..  I talked to the T-mobile agent on 3/19/24 this agent said my watch was being processed. I spoke with an agent on 3/20/24 and she said my watch would be sent to me on 3/22/24 but when I called on 3/21/24 the agent stated that there was no watch being processed.  I have not received a tracking number or description of my new watch. I now have to put another down payment on the new watch and I can't get the watch until the damaged watch is returned to the warehouse. I really thought T-mobile was a better company than this.

Completely scammed me into a contract saying that I’d pay $80 a month now my bill is $150 a month and no way for me to get out of it other than to pay them more money to break the contract. This can’t be legal. They flat out lied about providing a service for a price then changed the price. Scamming company that should be sued til its doors close. Hate this company with a passion.

So back in February I saw AT&T had a special on Samsung S24 Ultra AI phone. The phone with trade in was about 150 for a 1400 phone. So I thought I would do it using my old Note 10+ as a trade in. So got the phone and went into AT&T to have it set up and the Manager says our bad you’re not on the right rate plan if we move you to it the extra cost is 30 a month and the now 14 a month on the phone. I said nope cancel it I will go to T-Mobile for their deal on the same phone.
So got all cancelled and was refunded for fees I paid up front to AT&T.
I then went to T-Mobile and their deal was a little better on Samsung S24 Ultra AI phone. They required no trade in and would pay AT&T off 179 and give me 800 off the phone and 400 in credits which made the phone free except for taxes and fees which they all have. The guy said they will send an edoc to sign then all is a go. They sent the doc, I e-signed and T-Mobile sent a confirmation email acknowledging I signed. Then crickets for 5 days.
Called T-Mobile CS they said oh that order was cancelled because edocs were not signed. I said wait I signed them and here is the confirmation email from T-Mobile confirming that. They said Opps our bad must have been a system error. So, I had to go back to T-Mobile office and redo the order this time signing the edoc in front of the agent. He set up delivery to my house because the one I wanted Titanium Grey was not in stock and the closest store that had it was 60 miles away. Then the UPS issue starts.
So, UPS arranged delivery after 5 pm however they came at 9 am, so I rescheduled for 2-5pm the next day and they came at 9 am. I called UPS they said well the driver can change route to save fuel. I said but wait customers don’t know the driver is coming 5 or 6 hours ahead of a scheduled time. Her response Sorry, they will come Friday I said but no one will be there her response then they will send it back to T-Mobile. In the process UPS mishandled the phone and did not return it to T-Mobile.
So now third trip to T-Mobile again everything cancelled, and order reconstructed. Only this time I had it sent to work a warehouse where people do receiving all day and UPS is their main shipper. So, Tuesday 3/5/2024 I get confirmation package delivered at dock at 9:37 am. Went down to dock and no package. They said the auto sign has been happening since covid and driver still does it. The warehouse Manager said sometimes they do this then deliver it the next day. I called UPS they said dispatch would call within an hour, no call. So, I called again 2 hours later and again they said dispatch would call within an hour, however no call.
Wednesday 3/6/2024 still no package and no one can find it. Filed a claim with T-Mobile and UPS. Then dispatch called to get the information on the lost delivery.
Now T-Mobile billed me for a month of service on 2/27/2024 which even if I received the phone on the 2nd week remember they cancelled I would be getting billed a month for 2 weeks usage.
They have also not given me credit for the first charge for taxes and turnup of a phone I never received.
Now they will have to refund the second charge for taxes and turnup of the second phone I never received.
So I received a refund for the 110 usage bill for never using their service I guess it was for the privilege, never the less it was refunded. 
Then on 3/15/2024 I finally received a refund on the first phone order but still waiting on the second.
I called on 3/20/2024 and got the run around from two off shore agents requested over and over a supervisor or on shore customer service then the rep (Al) disconnected the call while I was on hold for a supervisor, right!
I called back another rep advised me the claim with T-Mobile was for the first phone no one had opened anything on the second phone. So now I must wait another 7 days for that claim.
She asked if I wanted to close account, I said hell yes but not until I get my refund. 
I thought AT&T had bad CS until I dealt with T-Mobile, T-Mobile is the champion of bad CS.

Customer service is lacking! I had a surgery, out of work on bedrest for a month. Called to explain to them why my first bill was unpaid. The next morning they disconnected my service. I will be returning their equipment and will advocate to everyone I meet to not waste their money on such a heartless company. Better off sticking with Verizon, at least they have a heart.

I was a very happy Sprint customer for almost 15 years. Their cellular service was great and their customer service was always outstanding. I was afraid of what would happen when T-Mobile was allowed to purchase Sprint, as I had heard T-Mobile's customer service was nowhere near as good as Sprint. My fears were realized when my family split our accounts when they qualified for a cheaper plan due to their age. I made THREE attempts after the split to change my plan to save myself $40/month, and the first two times each representative said they had changed the plan and still I was charged $110 for a single line. By the 3rd time, I was understandably upset and the customer service representative tried to argue with me about WHEN the change was made to my account. After 10 minutes of this, I told her to forget it and that I was changing services. I am extremely disappointed and angry at the $80 they managed to 'steal' from me over the last 2 months. I do NOT recommend T-Mobile...EVER. Score: F

I am a disabled Veteran and have TMobile service for approximately 15 months.  Since my paydays are different every month, my payments are late sometimes.  As a result of this, they have consistently suspended my services every month for the past 6 months.  I have recently tried to get them to give me a couple extra days to make a payment and keep my service on, but they will not.
for a telephone company they're trying, had issues with the free paramount + which always gets charged eventually and they did give me a credit towards my next bill. very nice.

This is the absolute worst cell service I have ever had. I’m counting down the days to finish my contract so I can leave. I’ve complained multiple times about dropped calls and lack of service and that even in my own home I only have service half the time, I’m always dismissed with “oh, it’s because you have an iPhone.” And never fix anything. Would highly suggest you never switch to this provider.

After 6 years of giving them an opportunity they turned out to be liers and a company that try to make their customers pay for their wrong doing.  Exchanged 2 phones under warranty and insurance and even a third party UPS confirmed it. They kept telling me they would take care of it on their end without interrupting my business for months and not do business like others after confirming the phones receipt (oddly they don't record it on their end, and UPS confirmed delivery) to fix their error trying to charge me over $2,100 for phones that where insured, paid and received, they disconnected/interrupting my services/business and excused this service where I had to call in for months and they would give their confirmation this would not happen, interrupting my time, business and ultimately having a serious negative impact.  Most of the time I traveled all over the US for work as this is a business line, I would often go without service.

I would give zero stars if I could. I had a business account with this company. While on phone with customers, the phone would drop calls. It became so frustrating that I ported my number to Verizon on 8/8/2023. By the way, I have not had any issues with Verizon. Any who, on September 23, I contacted T-Mobile because I received an unexpected phone bill. The representative stated that there were additional lines that were not ported. I was unaware of these lines because there wasn’t a SIM card or a phone for the lines in question. I asked that the account be canceled on 9/23. The representative stated that they would have to complete an investigation and someone would call me. Unfortunately, that never happened. On October 24th., I called T-Mobile because I received another bill that had accumulated additional charges. This issue was still not resolved due to their incompetent associates. On November 23rd, I contacted T-Mobile again to address the accumulated charges because I was billed on a phone that was not used for the months of August, September, October, and November. The line was finally disconnected in November due to nonpayment. When I spoke to a representative to inquire about the charges and who was responsible. He stated that the system noted a “future cancellation” I had to record this for my documentation. I did not ask for a future cancellation. I asked that the lines be canceled as of 9/23.

Now, the bill has went to collections with inaccurate data. Not only has T-Mobile incompetency ruined my business credit it has also forced me to pay an inaccurate bill which has been paid in full. If you’re considering T-Mobile as your phone carrier…. RUN!!!!!! pay a little more to have a peace of mind, exceptional customer service, and a technical/help support team.

I’ve been contacting T-Mobile since September it is NOW March and this issue has not been resolved. T-Mobile you can’t go around robbing customers without a gun. I’ve been a loyal customer for many years however, I’d never ever recommend your services nor will T-Mobile ever be my phone carrier again!

I am so thoroughly disappointed in T-Mobile right now. I used to be a proud T-Mobile customer and have even referred people to them in the past, but my experiences with them of late have been downright inane. 

I've paid the same balance with them three times: they didn't apply my first payment (even though it showed up in my bank account) so they sent me to collections; when I tried clear up the issue, I ended up paying the amount again. Then T-Mobile sent me to collections AGAIN and when I tried to prove once more that this was paid, the debt collector sided with T-Mobile and I ended up paying again. 

NOW, T-Mobile has sent me to a debt collector for the same balance AGAIN! And because I don't have my six digit code (on an account that's been closed for two years) T-Mobile can't help me over the phone. So, to get this thing cleared up AGAIN I have to go into a store - where the wait can be anywhere up to an hour or more - in order to get this thing resolved. I am not paying this for a fourth time and it irritates me that this keeps happening. 

I will not be referring people to T-Mobile anymore nor will I be a customer of theirs again.

Hello my name is Kenneth . I have been a customer for 11yrs. Since October of 2023 I have been having a problem with Assurant and the Tmobile Customer Service. I have been lied to on numerous time. I originally purchased a Samsung S20fe from the store in Woodbridge, VA. The phone has been exchanged out several time. The software had issues (ContinouslyBuffing), screen blacked out, and  when finally came around to the fifth time. The store ordered an replacement.  Got a text from Assurant  that the phone was on backorder and dont know when they would have one . I called and talked to your customer service about the issue. The representative told me to go to the store and get a replacement for free. Also, to give them the Samsung S20fe that had issues.I got the Samsung S23+ for my mother. I drove 150 miles to get the transaction done sine i work in Delaware and it was my day off. I paid the activation fee and was there for 4hours in the store. I was under the understanding that they were I was gonna owe $279 for new phone and accessories. Now told that the phone was $900 plus and they tried to add a new line on my account without my authorization. This transaction was noted on the account. Plus, that replacement never arrived. 
Now let's get to the New phone I bought in Newark,  DE. I bought this phone in November of 2023 while I was getting my car worked on. I had been waiting awhile to get a new one. So while I had time to burn as the car was getting worked on at the dealership. I paid the activation fee and get the accessories to protect the phone.was happy to get my new phone of Samsung Ultra 23.  On December 21. The phone fell when getting out my work truck. The Otterbox case cracked the back of phone and it shut off. I went back to the store and see what could be done. I had the phone for 21 days. They told me they couldn't help me and I had to go through Assurant. Funny thing is I'm 150 miles home from and my mother was having surgery on December 26, 2023. I got to a computer to start the claim and then it got complicated.  I texted the representative Chad K. He told I could go to Christiana Mall to get get replaced. Got up early  on December 22, 2023 and was at the mall at by 10:30am. They told me they couldn't help me. So, they let me use there phone in the store to start the claim. When it got to the payment status of the claim they hung up on me. I called the Customer Service at T-mobile to get them involved. I know Christmas was on December 25th and the phone wasn't gonna arrive until December 26th. I told them I wasn't gonna be home to receive it and gave them the address of the store in Newark, DE. When I went to pick the phone up @ 5:30pm. I was told that they couldn't accept the phone and sent it back to Assurant.  I called Usps and Assurant to see who had the phone. No one knew. I called Tmobile back and told them I needed the phone tonight or tomorrow December 27th for work. Assurant ad Tmobile promised the phone work arrive on that day. Long be hold it didn't arrive and more excuses were made. I was told by the representative to get another phone and I would have to pay for the old one.  The replacement didnt arrive until December 31, 2023 from Fedex. A neighbor found the package sitting outside and picked it up. I called several times after that with problems. They tried to add another number. To be frank and the point.  I was being billed for 4 lines and 4 protection plans. I have been fighting this matter since then. Tonight I went the store to get them to send the phone back to Assurant as told by the representative.  On January 21,Christina said, they would ship it back to them. The Store Representative open the bag and found that the phone was to be sent back Usps. The phone was never used and still in the security tape. Now, I was told by you Tmobile representative that you want me to pay for the old phone along with the new one. I understand one, but 2 no way.

T-Mobile has no competent person to contact if you have any problems with billing (lots) and their changes to business plans.

Verizon told us T-Mobile can’t continue billing us after their service was discontinued 1-29-24 but TM did.
We received a FINAL Bill in January 2024, which we paid, but new bills keep arriving incl 3-8-24.
If T-Mobile just paid their staff a decent wage, the managers could handle billing and plan complaints.
The Philippine people are probably paid $2 an hour so they are  angry and revengeful.
Just take a fraction on the millions spent in advertising and compensate your American workers to handle disputes. 
There are over 8 websites with negative reviews regarding T-Mobile  incl the Better Business Bureau.
Praying BBB will help us because our only option is to stop paying them and wreck our perfect credit.

Awful customer service. Customer service reps don't know current promotions and are only able to follow a script. Hours of my life I won't get back, and several hundred dollars wasted, due to their lack of timely response or my frustration of having to waste additional hours with little likelihood of resolving my concerns. They then referenced fine print to charge me an additional month of service for five lines, when only one line was active for a single day... 
The horror stories about their customer service are true!!! Consumers beware!
They have lost a reliable customer for life - no more T-Mobile for me.

Home Internet started out as a good deal when first signing up but soon became a lackluster then bad purchase..
After the first several months, download and upload speeds decreased month after month. Signal strength was often poor and increasingly difficult to achieve 4 or 5 bars even through my giant 8th floor living room window. The gateway device also malfunctioned during my ownership and the screen died. Then last year they removed the AutoPay discount for credit cards, leading to an extra $5 charge for slower speeds.
Finally this past month I had to cancel the service after moving, which was a cumbersome process involving multiple long calls to customer service and then several stores not accepting the return of the gateway device and driving miles to the correct store.
Finally, as a transgender person who has legally changed my name and had my correct name listed with T-Mobile, I was harassed by a customer service agent whom deliberately escalated an antagonistic conversation with me for 10 minutes, when all I wanted to do was just confirm that my account was closed out. I was eventually forced by the agent to list a prior and incorrect name 'deadname' which is no longer even a legal name for any person, just to close my account. This agent was very rude, stubborn and incompetent - as multiple information and identifiers could clearly identify and close my account without bringing up some dead name from years ago - and I felt that this transphobic, sadistically humiliating agent just capped off a bad experience with T-Mobile overall. 2/5 Stars.

DO NOT GET TMOBILE PHONE OR INTERNET!  Customer service is horrible, they transfer you to one department, that department then transfers you back cause they say that you should not have been transfered in the first place. The rebates they offer are a scam. They have you jump through hoops to get it yet you still don't receive it. The customer care supervisors (Cliff) lie to you even though everything is recorded. Then they try to blame another company (amazon) until you tell and send them the email from amazon stating they have absolutely nothing to do with said promotion. Stay far away from this company, unless you like wasting time talking to them only to get nothing accomplished!

Which we agreed to it.  But when January came my bill was 232.+, no where near what the guy told me. My opinion about T-mobile, they tell you one thing at the store to get your business, but the reality is another,  and that is how T.Monile fraud their customers out of their money.  This is the way this organization works because the same thing happened to me two years ago. So please be in the look out for this T-Mobile organization because there is never an special with them, they will lure in with this promotions that don't work and you get stuck with a big bill. Their customer service is not good and on top of it they work from some where in Lala land and sometimes you don't understand what they said.

I am writing this letter of complaint because of the unsatisfactory customer service treatment that I received at your store located at 3204 Pennsylvania Avenue SE. I visited the store on Saturday March 2, 2024 and spoke with one of the store representatives. I explained to her that my phone was not working, it had been not holding a charge and finally, went all the way down. She checked the phone and confirmed that the phone was not functioning. She prompted me to make a claim and provided me with a phone to call the claims Department. I proceeded accordingly, and the claim was approved with a $49.00 deductible paid.  My wife opted to have the phone sent to the store for pick-up. I was informed that the phone had arrived at the store and proceeded to pick the phone up at around 10:45 AM on Monday March 4, 2024. The store representative asked a few questions regarding my claim, which I thought was odd, since I had already been through that process. The store representatives requested my identification to verify my identification. After a while, she told me that she couldn’t scan the phone into the system, only the manger had the credentials to take such actions. Therefore, I returned home and went back to the store around 12:35 PM. I was told that the manager had not arrived and was told to return around 2:30. I asked if a regional manger can perform the activity necessary for me to retrieve my phone. I was told no; only the store manger could perform this activity. I waited approximately 35 additional minutes for the store manager to arrive. When I returned to the store, there was a waiting period and then additional questions were asked by the store representative. After a while, the store representative told me that the claim was denied. I was very perplexed because the claim had already been approved. Therefore, I requested to contact the claims department to ascertain what had transpired between my claim being approved to the store denying my claim. The T-Mobile claim representative spoke with the manger, Abdul, because she said that the claim had been approved. After a few more moments, the store representative began to asked additional questions and requested my identification again, now I am not only perplexed, confused but also annoyed. Finally, Abdul informed me that the claim had been denied and proceeded to cancel the order.

I am thankful that the customer service department responded by re-ordering the phone and refund my deductible. However, I remain concern regarding this unsatisfactory level of customer service, since this is the T-Mobile store that is closest to my house. I am hoping that this issue is corrected, that I may not experience such horrible services the next time I visit this location.

Feel free to contact me should you need to discuss this matter.

We canceled our account and 3 lines (one of which was never in use by us or anyone else) and T Mobile continued to charge us. When I called to complain they refused to refund us. I’ve since had to file fraud with my bank, and will be pursuing legal action. Long story short, unless you want horrible service and to pay for something you no longer use, avoid T Mobile.

WHY DOES THE HORRIBLE ROBOT THAT ANSWERS T-MOBILE'S 611 NOT RECOGNIZE THAT I JUST PAID MY BILL?  WHY DOES IT ASK, "DO YOU WANT TO REFILL YOUR ACCOUNT NOW?" WHEN I ALREADY DID?!  WHY HASN'T T-MOBILE EMAILED AND/OR TEXTED ME A PAYMENT RECEIPTTO ACKNOWLEDGE THAT MY T-MOBILE BILL HAS BEEN PAID?  WHY MUST T-MOBILE CUSTOMERS GIVE THE PHONE ROBOT OUR ACCOUNT PIN NUMBER AS WELL AS GIVING IT TO A HUMAN T-MOBILE EMPLOYEE WHO COULD POTENTIALLY STEAL OR ALTER CUSTOMER PIN NUMBERS? HUMANS ARE GENERALLY WILDLY UNPREDICTABLE BY NATURE. T-MOBILE SHOULD MAKE THE ABOVE- MENTIONED COMPANY CHANGES, AND YOU SHOULD PLEASE ENSURE THAT THOSE CHANGES ARE MADE.  FUTILELY I SAY THANK YOU, BUT I SADLY DON'T THINK ANYTHING WILL CHANGE, CERTAINLY NOT FOR THE BETTER IN THIS TECH-DRIVEN WORLD.😟

After being a customer for many years with this company, they simply do not care. I’ve been paying off a barely working IPhone for 4 years. I have to pay 99.00 to trade it in for the same obsolete model despite signing up for their buy one get one free plan years ago. Due to the fact we’ve upgraded - that was cancelled. Obviously, most people want to upgrade. 

The company preys on people’s desire to be trendy and in. They prey on their misfortune, and their absentminded selves. They bought Metro PCS so nope, cannot go there and pay less, they bought Sprint, so now cannot go there and not deal with these crazy corporate games since they are TMobile now. 

We need phones because it’s 2024 sadly. So we put up with it. They know this. 

There are never any offers local to people in the Northeast for the T-Mobile Tuesdays hardly. There are definitely no offers for loyal customers. No discounts nothing. You want that fancy new phone? You’ll have to pay for it for 4 years in small payments and it’s a never ending high bill. 

I’ve had to replace my daughters phone about 7 times and pay a fee every time of a whopping 99.00. The Assurian insurance is terrible. If T mobile is such a big company, why not just walk in the store, pay the deductible and exchange phones there if we are to pay the high price? Twice Assurian claimed they lost the old phones we sent in-  threatening to charge us for not sending in the (very broken phones) only for many calls later to have “found” them once shown proof of tracking. It’s truly corporate greed and being treated like a number at its finest. 

I’ve been told “if we do this for one person we’d have to do it for everyone!” 

The reason for my final upset - I’ve fallen on hard times these last few months and cannot pay my insanely expensive bill. Our phones will be shut off because they want to argue with me over the fact I cannot pay the last two bills and they will get their money eventually. They are a multibillion dollar company. They make you promise to pay on a Sunday so if you get any money on Friday then it won’t be in that day. You don’t realise this. They do though. 

They also won’t let a relative or friend pay in the store either or let you pay with an expired ID as I don’t have money to get a new one yet. 

I’m a responsible person. I had a job. I ended up working for a company who replaced a higher up with a very unprofessional individual to put it nicely - and I left for my safety. I have not been able to get a job since. I’ve been living on savings and some I will get back soon. T mobile cannot wait??? I have proof of this. They are a very wealthy company. 

This never has happened we have always paid. So clearly something personal is going on. They do not care. They should really have honoured their buy one get one on the phones too but that is not happening either because my daughter wanted to upgrade. 

So that’s my experience with T-Mobile and that is what happens when a company becomes a monopoly. Thank you.

Coming from a $500 bill with Verizon Wireless, down to $207 with T-Mobile is incredible! I have been treated like a valuable customer since day one. No company is perfect. No company is trying to be perfect either. I appreciate the way T-Mobile has handled my business.

RUN FROM THEM!
This company is a thief!  We cancelled our service and they tried to say it was cancelled 9 days after billing cycle started.  This is a lie.  Also, we were getting Paramount Plus through TMobile.  They were double billing this and would not correct!  So well after cancellation they took the full month payment automatically (the second we cancelled service they cancelled our entire account so we were unable to cancel autopay).  They are refusing to refund our money or guarantee no further charges even though account is cancelled.

Where do I start?

My wifi has been down for three days and counting, which is also how long I have tried unsuccessfully to reach someone who can help. Oh yeah, they also have me locked out of my system, and I can get no help with that either. Chat says they need to access my account and apparently they cannot help. Voice line says there's a wait, but they never call back when you request it. I am DONE.

T-mobile telephone customer service is extremely frustrating! The agents are often difficult to understand and they have to keep putting you on hold. I was on the phone for over an hour and a half with waiting and being put on hold numerous times. I hope to NEVER have to call them again!!!

Stay away run, run, run!! When 1st signed up promotion $750 off per phone (2 phones) savings $1500 after 7 months l I paid phones off in full. I bought 2 new phones they gave me trade in value and I paid cash for these and ths promotional discount. I stayed another 4 months until sneaky price increase so I switched providers. Following month received bill almost $500 I blew a gasket. What happens those so called "PROMOTIONS" are only if you stay with them for 24 months (it's in the fine print). If you get $1000 off per phone don't stay 24 months you pay that    BACK!!!  LONG STORY SHORT.....BEWARE OF TMOBILES PROMOTIONS and other fees I don't even want to type out.

They are cheating with Customer and direct pay charging more and stelling my money from my bank account. When called Customer service call goes some other country and with poor English and hard to communicate

The company does not value its customers as they claim. They treated me like crap after been with them for over a decade. The insurance company they use (Assurant) is equally a fraud. They claimed not to receive my old damaged cell phone I sent back to them during a claim. They were charging me $1,100 for the no-good cell phone. Is this how phone and insurance companies suppose to conduct business with their loyal customer? I am going to file against them and switch carrier.

Over-the phone internet T-Mobile rates change within the hour!
I telephoned the Pembroke Pines/I-75 store, and Ernesto told me that the internet rate would be $50. I went into the store, filled in all the paperwork, had a soft credit check, lifted my credit freeze on 3 credit bureaus, and then Ernesto announced that “Oh, they just changed the rate a few days ago. The rate is $65.”
I wasted 1 hour because of Ernesto’s lie! If you have an hour to waste, telephone T-Mobil, listen to a non-existent rate, then go into the store, and you’ll hear the real rate—which will be higher than the one he quoted.  
He also told me that I could have a 2-week trial period. But that was over the over-the-telephone rate. Maybe it will be a 2-day trial period if I go into the store. Who knows?
Welcome to T-Mobil—where you get a low internet price over the phone, but by the time to get INTO the store, you get the higher price! 
The store was empty. I should have known. Everyone is smart enough to stay away from Ernesto’s lies. You have been warned!
Scammed by Tmobile. They don't honor their promos or their trade in offers then they charge to call customer service where they make false promises and never fix problems.
If could give no start, I would. The worst service, worst coverage. I would never come back to them, and wont' recommend anyone.

Tmobile is absolute garbage. Overcharged the shtt out of us. Then promised to correct the bill over a month ago. Never corrected it. I've contacted customer service dozens of times and had repeated promises that it would be corrected. Last night they said not to worry and our service would not be interupted since I have said I am not paying the bill until it is correct. This morning our service was disconnected and even more additional fees added for the service disconnect. They asked me to pay the full amount to restore service now. This has been the worst service I've dealt with from any cell service. Complete bullshtt.

Returned a watch. They kept charging on the line. when we caught on we had already payed for that line a total of $225. they denied it was returned. They refused refund and if the line gets cancelled, they will cancel the rebate and we would have to pay $93 remaining on the watch.
